mcqs.question: In a singly linked list, each node contains:

options.text: Data and a pointer to the next node
Data and pointers to both previous and next nodes

mcqs.explanation: A singly linked list node contains two fields: DATA (the actual value) and NEXT (pointer/reference to the next node in the list).

mcqs.question: What is the time complexity of inserting a node at the beginning of a linked list?
mcqs.explanation: Insertion at the beginning only requires creating a new node, pointing it to current head, and updating head. No traversal needed, so O(1).

mcqs.question: Which of the following is NOT an advantage of linked lists over arrays?
mcqs.explanation: Linked lists do NOT provide random access to elements. Array elements can be accessed directly using indices, but linked list requires traversal from head.

mcqs.question: In a circular linked list, the last node points to:
mcqs.explanation: In a circular linked list, the last node's next pointer points back to the first node (head), creating a circular structure.

mcqs.question: What will be the output of traversing a singly linked list?
mcqs.explanation: A singly linked list can only be traversed in forward direction from head to tail because each node only has a pointer to the next node.

mcqs.question: Which data structure uses linked list implementation internally?
mcqs.explanation: Stacks can be efficiently implemented using linked lists. Arrays are a separate data structure, heaps typically use arrays, hash tables use arrays internally.

mcqs.question: What is the worst-case time complexity to search an element in a singly linked list?
mcqs.explanation: In the worst case, the element is at the last position or not present, requiring traversal of all n nodes, hence O(n) time complexity.

mcqs.question: In a doubly linked list, each node has how many pointers?
mcqs.explanation: A doubly linked list node has two pointers: PREV (points to previous node) and NEXT (points to next node), plus one data field.

mcqs.question: What happens when you delete the first node from a linked list?

options.text: Head pointer is moved to the second node

mcqs.explanation: When deleting the first node, the head pointer is updated to point to the second node (head->next), effectively making it the new first node.

mcqs.question: Which algorithm is used to detect a cycle in a linked list?
mcqs.explanation: Floyd's Cycle Detection Algorithm (also called Tortoise and Hare algorithm) uses two pointers moving at different speeds to detect loops.

mcqs.question: What is the main disadvantage of a singly linked list compared to a doubly linked list?
mcqs.explanation: Singly linked lists only have NEXT pointers, making backward traversal impossible. Doubly linked lists have PREV pointers enabling bidirectional traversal.

mcqs.question: In C, which function is used to allocate memory for a linked list node?
mcqs.explanation: In C, malloc() (memory allocation) is used to dynamically allocate memory for nodes. It returns a pointer to the allocated memory block.

mcqs.question: What is a dummy header node in a linked list?

options.text: A placeholder node at the beginning without data
The last node in the list

mcqs.explanation: A dummy header node is a special node placed at the beginning that doesn't store actual data but simplifies insertion/deletion operations at the start.

mcqs.question: What is the space complexity for storing n elements in a linked list?
mcqs.explanation: A linked list with n nodes requires O(n) space for data plus O(n) for pointers, so overall space complexity is O(n).

mcqs.question: How do you insert a node at the end of a singly linked list?

options.text: Traverse to end and add new node

mcqs.explanation: To insert at the end, you must traverse the entire list to find the last node (where next is NULL), then set its next pointer to the new node. Time: O(n).

mcqs.question: Which operation is NOT O(1) in a singly linked list?
mcqs.explanation: Insertion at tail requires traversal to find the last node (O(n)). Insertion at head and deletion at head are O(1) as they only require pointer updates.

mcqs.question: What type of linked list is best for maintaining a playlist that can be played forward and backward?
mcqs.explanation: A doubly linked list is ideal as it has both PREV and NEXT pointers, allowing navigation in both forward and backward directions.

mcqs.question: What is the time complexity to find the length of a linked list?
mcqs.explanation: To find length, you must traverse all nodes from head until NULL, counting nodes. This takes O(n) time complexity.

mcqs.question: In Python, what is used to represent a linked list node?

options.text: Class with data and next attributes

mcqs.explanation: In Python, a node is typically created using a class with two attributes: data (to store value) and next (to store reference to next node).

mcqs.question: What happens to the memory when a node is deleted from a linked list?

options.text: Memory must be explicitly freed (free() in C)
Memory becomes garbage automatically in C

mcqs.explanation: In C, memory allocated with malloc() must be explicitly freed using free(). In languages like Java/Python, garbage collection handles deallocation.
